title: Ensure group root is the only GID 0 group

prose.en: Ensure group root is the only GID 0 group

description.en: The `groupmod` command can be used to specify which group the `root` group belongs to. This affects permissions of files that are group owned by the `root` group.

rationale.en: Using GID 0 for the `root` group helps prevent `root` group owned files from accidentally becoming accessible to non\-privileged users.

audit.en:
Run the following command to set the `root` group's GID to `0`:  

Remove any groups other than the `root` group with GID 0 or assign them a new GID if appropriate.

```
# groupmod -g 0 root
```

remediation.en:
Run the following command to set the `root` group's GID to `0`:  

Remove any groups other than the `root` group with GID 0 or assign them a new GID if appropriate.

```
# groupmod -g 0 root
```

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1, CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
